name: Pad Thai Stir-Fried Rice Ribbon Noodles
recipeIngredient: Medium shrimp, shelled and deveined (1/4 lb)
Boneless chicken breast, half-skinned and thinly sliced
Preserved radish, chopped and soaked in warm water for 10 minutes, drained (optional)
Chicken broth or water (2 tablespoons, as needed)
Green onions, trimmed and cut into 2-inch lengths (4)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place noodles in a bowl and cover with warm water; let soak about 15 minutes, or until soft and pliable. Drain; set aside.
2. Set a wok over medium-high heat. When hot, add 2 tablespoons of oil.
3. Add shrimp and chicken; stir-fry until shrimp turns bright orange and chicken turns white, about 30 seconds. Set aside.
4. Reheat wok over medium heat. Add the remaining oil and garlic; brown gently (about 20 seconds).
5. Increase heat to medium-high. Add bean sauce, tomato paste, fish sauce, vinegar, and sugar; stir until sugar dissolves.
6. Increase heat to high. Break one egg into the sauce; beat for 3-4 times. Let cook until egg is slightly set but still moist, about 20 seconds.
7. Repeat with second and third eggs. There should be tiny flecks of egg peeking through.
8. Add optional radish, dried shrimp, tofu, chili flakes, softened noodles, and 1 handful of bean sprouts to the hot wok. Toss and lift noodles until tender and no longer stiff, about 3-4 minutes.
9. Add chicken broth (2 tablespoons at a time) if noodles seem dry, green onions, and reserved shrimp/chicken mixture; toss for 30 seconds to reheat.
10. Transfer to a platter and serve with sprinkling of chopped peanuts, chili flakes to taste, and fresh coriander leaves. Arrange remaining bean sprouts and lime wedges around noodles. Squeeze lime over noodles before eating.
11. Serve warm or at room temperature.
